successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveRequest, LeaveRequestStatus, LeaveType, CreateLeaveRequestDTO, and UpdateLeaveRequestStatusDTO with the architecture-defined shapes.

src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and exports AuditRecord; src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports AuditRecordRepository and PostgreSqlAuditRecordRepository with PostgreSQL-backed create persistence.

src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports LeaveRequestRepository and PostgreSqlLeaveRequestRepository with create, findById, updateStatus, and delete methods matching the specified interfaces.
All state-changing LeaveRequest repository methods (create, updateStatus, delete) write an AuditRecord through AuditRecordRepository.
tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts exists and verifies create, read, updateStatus, and delete repository behavior using Vitest.

constraints: Use the repository pattern: all database access must be encapsulated behind repository interfaces.
Implement PostgreSQL-backed persistence using the provided SQL schema and pg Pool dependencies.
All state-changing operations must create an audit record, satisfying the audit-record requirement.
Keep scope limited to LeaveRequest and AuditRecord domain models, repositories, and the specified unit test.
Respect module dependency direction: leave may depend on audit, but no reverse dependency from audit to leave.
No unhandled promise rejections; all async errors must be caught and handled appropriately.
Do not introduce workflow logic, approval flows, notifications, balances, policies, employee integration, RBAC endpoints, or API layers in this phase.
Repository interfaces and exported types must match the architecture-defined contracts.
outOfScope: Leave request submission, approval, rejection, or cancellation workflow services
Notification domain model, repositories, or event generation
LeaveBalance domain model, repositories, or balance deduction logic
LeavePolicy domain model, repositories, or rule evaluation
Employee integration and manager relationship logic
API endpoints, request validation layers, and RBAC enforcement implementations
Database migrations or schema changes beyond assuming the provided schema
Phase 2 through Phase 7 functionality described as deferred

ambiguities.description: The unit test requirement specifies CRUD verification but does not define whether tests should use a real PostgreSQL instance, a test database, or mocked Pool/AuditRecordRepository dependencies.
options: Use mocked PostgreSQL and audit repository dependencies for isolated unit tests
Use an integration-style test against a real or ephemeral PostgreSQL database
